ruleset.name: Trophy Dark
ruleset.edition: Trophy Dark (Jesse Ross, 2019 — Codex publication; mechanics descend from Jason Tocci's 24XX SRD)
ruleset.license: Trophy Dark mechanics by Jesse Ross / Hedgemaze Press. 24XX SRD (Jason Tocci) is CC BY 4.0. This ruleset file is a clean-room re-implementation of the resolution mechanic for the Marinara-RPG framework; no setting prose, treasure-hunter pitches, ruin descriptions, or other Hedgemaze copyrightable text is reproduced. Buy the game at hedgemazepress.com.
ruleset.summary: One-shot horror RPG: treasure hunters in a haunted wood, doomed from the start. Each character has Pursuits (light-die professions) and Backgrounds (light-die histories). Risk rolls mix the character's light d6s with situational dark d6s; the highest light face determines outcome (5+ clean, 4 cost, 1-3 devil's bargain), and any dark die higher than the highest light die inflicts Ruin. APPROXIMATED here as a stance-modal-pool: stance=light succeeds OVER Risk (3) to mirror the 4+ band, stance=dark fails UNDER Risk to mirror the corruption-overrules-light cascade. See gm-agent.md for the deviation note — true Trophy Dark wants a dual-pool with comparison, which stance-modal-pool cannot express in one roll.

dice.notation: Pd6 stance=LIGHT|DARK vs Risk (light dice = your Pursuits + Backgrounds; dark dice = GM-assigned)

stances.label: DARK
stances.description: Roll the GM-assigned dark dice for this scene. A dark die UNDER Risk (1-2) is mundane menace; faces 4-6 represent the wood's corruption gaining ground. Use the gm-agent's dark-overrule guidance when narrating, since stance-modal-pool cannot express 'highest dark beats highest light' in a single roll.

stances.label: LIGHT
stances.description: Roll your character's light dice (1 die per applicable Pursuit, +1 per applicable Background, +1 per Devil's Bargain accepted, +1 per Helping ally). A face OVER Risk (4-6) is a success; the outcomeTier walks this success count to clean / costly / devil's bargain.

exactMatch.name: EDGE OF RUIN
exactMatch.effect: A die rolling EXACTLY Risk (3) is a hair's-breadth moment. It does NOT count as success on its own, but on a light roll the GM should flavor the narration with 'the wood almost takes you' — a free reminder that Ruin is coming. On a dark roll, it counts as a near-miss the GM banks for the next scene.

outcomeTiers.label: devils-bargain
outcomeTiers.narration: No light die came up 4+. The action fails as attempted; GM offers a Devil's Bargain (mark Ruin, accept a complication, sacrifice an item, or trade something the wood wants) to convert this into a costly success. Decline and the failure stands. Trophy Dark RAW: tier 1-3 on highest light.

outcomeTiers.label: at-a-cost
outcomeTiers.narration: Exactly one light die came up 4+ — and likely it was the 4 itself. You succeed AT A COST: harm, complication, a piece of yourself left in the wood. Now check dark dice (GM): any dark face higher than the highest light face? If yes, mark Ruin. Trophy Dark RAW: tier 4 on highest light.

outcomeTiers.label: clean
outcomeTiers.narration: Multiple light dice came up 4+ — at least one was likely a 5 or 6. The action succeeds cleanly. Even so, check dark dice: if any dark face exceeded your highest light face, mark Ruin anyway. The wood corrupts even clean wins. Trophy Dark RAW: tier 5-6 on highest light.

attributes.name: Risk
attributes.description: Fixed Trophy Dark threshold (3). Faces 4-6 succeed on light dice; faces 1-3 fail. Held at min=max=3 to model the system's invariant Risk line — Trophy Dark does not vary this stat per character (unlike L&F's Number). Player should not edit.

skills.name: Burglar
skills.description: Pursuit. Climbing, picking locks, slipping into places that don't want you. When the GM agrees this Pursuit applies, contribute 1 light die to the Risk roll.

skills.name: Brute
skills.description: Pursuit. Strength, intimidation, smashing through obstacles. Contributes 1 light die when applicable.

skills.name: Sneak
skills.description: Pursuit. Stealth, deception, going unseen. Contributes 1 light die when applicable.

skills.name: Scholar
skills.description: Pursuit. Lore, languages, reading the unreadable. Contributes 1 light die when applicable.

skills.name: Cursed
skills.description: Background. A doom rides on your shoulders. When the curse is relevant (often: when something supernatural blocks the path), contribute 1 light die to the Risk roll.

skills.name: Doomed
skills.description: Background. You know how you die — you've seen the wood-shape of it. Contributes 1 light die when the doom-vision applies.

skills.name: Haunted
skills.description: Background. Something dead follows you. Contributes 1 light die when communing with the dead, surviving fear, or facing the wood's spirits.

skills.name: Soldier
skills.description: Background. You bled in a war and came home wrong. Contributes 1 light die for combat, command, and reading other violent people.

skills.name: Smuggler
skills.description: Background. Routes, contacts, the trick of moving forbidden things. Contributes 1 light die for navigation, contraband, and lies told fast.

derivedStats.name: Ruin
derivedStats.formula: ticks accumulated from failed Risk rolls and Devil's Bargains; at Ruin = 6 the character is consumed by the wood

derivedStats.name: Burdens
derivedStats.formula: narrative tracker for accepted Devil's Bargains the GM is holding over the character; not a number that drives dice math

diceTagFormat.template: [mrrp-roll: ruleset=trophy-dark, stance={stance}, stat=Risk, statValue={statValue}, pool={pool}, dice=[{diceCsv}], successes={successes}, exactMatches={exactMatches}, tier={tier}{narrationHookPart}]
diceTagFormat.example: [mrrp-roll: ruleset=trophy-dark, stance=light, stat=Risk, statValue=3, pool=3, dice=[2,4,5], successes=2, exactMatches=0, tier=clean, narrationHook=]

lorebook.name: Trophy Dark Rules Reference
lorebook.description: Keyword-triggered rules reference for the GM agent running Trophy Dark (Jesse Ross) one-shots in the Marinara-RPG framework's approximated stance-modal-pool mode. Each entry surfaces only when its keys appear in recent narration or player input, so the GM gets the right rule at the right moment without inflating every prompt.

entries.name: Risk Roll Resolution
entries.content: Trophy Dark uses RISK rolls. Light dice (the character's) and dark dice (the GM's) are rolled together; the highest light face determines outcome on the 4+ band, and any dark face higher than the highest light face inflicts Ruin. The Marinara-RPG ruleset approximates this with a stance-modal-pool. Stance LIGHT counts dice OVER Risk (3) — mirrors the 4+ success band. Stance DARK counts dice UNDER Risk (3) — mirrors the wood's mundane menace. The stance approximation cannot fully express RAW's dark-overrules-light comparison; the gm-agent.md flags this and instructs the GM to run a second mental check after a stance-LIGHT roll: if the highest dark die rolled (or imagined) would exceed the highest light face, narrate Ruin.

entries.name: Outcome Tiers (Devil's Bargain, At a Cost, Clean)
entries.content: Count successes (light faces of 4 or higher in the approximation). 0 successes = DEVIL'S BARGAIN: the action fails as attempted; the GM offers a bargain — mark Ruin, accept a complication, sacrifice an item, or trade something the wood wants — to convert this into a costly success. Decline and the failure stands. 1 success = AT A COST: the action succeeds, but harm or complication lands; a piece of the hunter is left in the wood. 2+ successes = CLEAN: the action succeeds without harm — but the GM still checks the dark dice. If any dark face exceeded the highest light face, mark Ruin anyway. Trophy Dark corrupts even clean wins.

entries.name: Ruin Track
entries.content: Each hunter has a RUIN track from 0 to 6. Mark Ruin on: failed Risk rolls where a dark face exceeds the highest light face; accepted Devil's Bargains; specific narrative beats the GM telegraphs in advance (the wood always demands payment for what it gives). At Ruin 6, the character is CONSUMED BY THE WOOD — narrated as a corruption that takes them whole, transforming them into something the surviving hunters will have to face. The retire-by-corruption is the game's central engine; players should feel the track tightening every scene. Ruin does NOT clear during a one-shot. Trophy Dark is a doomed-tone game.

entries.name: Devil's Bargain
entries.content: When a hunter is about to fail or wants extra dice, the GM may offer a DEVIL'S BARGAIN: the hunter trades something to the wood in exchange for a die or a success. Standard trades: mark Ruin (1-2 points depending on stakes), sacrifice an item (especially treasure), accept a lasting complication (the wood marks the hunter physically or psychically), reveal a secret the hunter has been keeping. A good Devil's Bargain feels tempting AND costly; if the player accepts every bargain without hesitation, the bargains aren't biting hard enough. Always describe the cost in concrete fictional terms before the player decides — Trophy Dark's horror comes from informed consent to ruin.

entries.name: Pursuits and Backgrounds
entries.content: Each hunter has PURSUITS (light-die professions: Burglar, Brute, Sneak, Scholar — pick one or two at creation) and BACKGROUNDS (light-die histories: Cursed, Doomed, Haunted, Soldier, Smuggler, more — pick one or two). When the GM agrees a Pursuit applies to the action, the hunter rolls +1 light die. Same for an applicable Background. The GM has wide latitude — a Scholar in a treasure-vault gets the die; a Scholar fleeing a dire wolf does not. Background dice often carry tonal weight: a Cursed hunter's curse always finds a way to be relevant; a Doomed hunter's death-vision shapes how the GM frames their fate scenes.

entries.name: Rituals (The Other Sub-System)
entries.content: Trophy Dark rituals are a SEPARATE sub-system — they are not Risk rolls. A hunter casting a ritual gathers ingredients, follows a specific procedure described in the ritual's text, and then narrates the casting. The GM judges whether the procedure was followed; on success, the effect manifests; on failure, the wood twists it. Most rituals always cost Ruin (often 1-2 points) regardless of outcome — knowledge of the wood corrupts you. Rituals are designed by the GM (or pulled from published Codex zines), not generated by a system at play time. When a player wants to cast something improvised, the GM should EITHER refuse and ask them to find a written ritual, OR treat the improvisation as a Risk roll with a steep Devil's Bargain attached.

entries.name: Tone: Doomed Treasure Hunters in a Haunted Wood
entries.content: Trophy Dark is a ONE-SHOT horror game about treasure hunters who entered a haunted wood and will not all return. The treasure pitch is the hook ('the lost vault of the silver-tongue queen, said to ride on the back of an iron stag'); the wood is the antagonist. The game arc is 3-5 scenes called INCURSIONS, each ramping pressure on the Ruin tracks. The expected ending is 1-2 hunters consumed, 0-2 hunters fled with partial treasure, 0-1 hunters dragging the prize home changed forever. Aim for that ratio. Trophy Dark does NOT continue across sessions — start a new one-shot, with new hunters, in a new wood. The doom is the genre.
